extra.message: User input from params["id"] is directly concatenated into a SQL query string passed to cursor.execute(). No sanitization or parameterization is performed, allowing full SQL injection including UNION-based extraction, boolean-based blind injection, and time-based blind injection.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User input from params["comment"] is directly string-formatted into a SQL INSERT statement without parameterization. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL, e.g. closing the string and appending malicious SQL clauses.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: The login endpoint at /login concatenates params["password"] directly into a SQL query string without parameterization. Although params["username"] is passed through re.sub(r"[^\w]", "", ...), the password field receives no sanitization at all, enabling login bypass via SQL injection (e.g., password=' OR '1' LIKE '1).

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled params["domain"] is concatenated directly into the shell command string "nslookup " + params["domain"] and passed to subprocess.run() with shell=True. An attacker can inject arbitrary OS commands by supplying values like 'example.com; rm -rf /' or using backtick/pipe/semicolon injection.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User-supplied input from params["object"] is base64-decoded and passed directly to pickle.loads(). Python's pickle deserialization can execute arbitrary code via the __reduce__ method, allowing remote code execution. An attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload that runs any OS command.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: User-controlled params["path"] is passed directly to open(os.path.abspath(...)) without any restriction on which files may be accessed. os.path.abspath() does not prevent traversal — it merely resolves the absolute path. An attacker can read any file the process has permission to read (e.g., ?path=../../etc/passwd). If the value contains '://' it is instead fetched via urllib.request.urlopen(), introducing SSRF as well.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: When params["path"] contains '://', the application calls urllib.request.urlopen(params["path"]) with no validation of the target URL. An attacker can force the server to make requests to arbitrary internal or external hosts, including file://, http://, ftp:// schemes, enabling SSRF to probe internal services or exfiltrate data.

cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

extra.message: User-supplied XML from params["xml"] is parsed with lxml.etree.XMLParser(load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, no_network=False). This configuration explicitly enables external entity resolution and network access, allowing classic XXE attacks: reading arbitrary local files via file:// entities, port scanning, and potential SSRF via http:// entities in DTD declarations.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference ('XXE')

extra.message: User input from params["name"] is directly interpolated into an XPath expression via string formatting: ".//user[name/text()='%s']" % params["name"]. No escaping or parameterization is applied, allowing an attacker to inject XPath predicates to bypass filtering or extract arbitrary node data from the XML document (e.g., supplying ' or '1'='1 to match all users).

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')

extra.message: The application reads a file or URL specified by user-controlled params["include"] (local file or remote URL via urllib) and passes its contents to exec(). This allows remote code execution: an attacker can provide a URL pointing to attacker-controlled Python code, or a local file path containing Python code, which will be executed with the server process's privileges.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled params["redir"] is inserted directly into a meta refresh redirect tag without validation: "<meta http-equiv=\"refresh\" content=\"0; url=%s\"/>". There is no check that the URL is relative or belongs to a trusted domain. An attacker can supply an arbitrary external URL to redirect victims to phishing sites.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: SQL query results (from the user-injectable ?id= parameter) are rendered directly into HTML without HTML-escaping: "%s" % value. If an attacker stores XSS payloads via the SQL injection, or if a database value contains HTML tags, those will be executed in the victim's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-Site Scripting')

extra.message: User-submitted comment data is stored in the database via the vulnerable INSERT at line 50 and then retrieved and rendered directly into HTML at line 54 without any HTML encoding: "%s" % value. An attacker can submit a comment containing a

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-Site Scripting')

extra.message:
The HTML_PREFIX constant includes inline JavaScript that reads the URL fragment (document.location.hash) for a 'lang=' parameter and passes it to document.write() without sanitization: document.write('
' + decodeURIComponent(document.location.hash.substring(index + 5)) + '
'). An attacker can craft a URL with a fragment containing HTML/script tags that will be written to the DOM and executed, constituting DOM-based XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-Site Scripting')

extra.message: User input from params["v"] is inserted unescaped into the HTML response via regex substitution: re.sub(r"(v)[^<]+()", r"\g<1>%s\g<2>" % params["v"], HTML_POSTFIX). An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript by supplying a crafted value for the ?v= parameter, enabling reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-Site Scripting')

extra.message: The /users.json endpoint reflects the user-supplied params["callback"] value directly as a JSONP wrapper without validation or escaping: "%s(" % params["callback"]. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript as the callback name (e.g., ?callback=alert(1)//), resulting in XSS when the response is loaded in a browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-Site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled params["size"] is passed to int() and used directly to control the number and size of string allocations: "#" * int(params["size"]) for _ in range(int(params["size"])). This results in O(n^2) memory and CPU usage. Supplying a large value (e.g., ?size=1000000) can exhaust server memory and CPU, causing denial of service.

cwe: CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption

extra.message: The USERS_XML constant at line 11 contains plaintext passwords for multiple user accounts hardcoded directly in the source file (e.g., admin:7en8aiDoh!, dricci:12345, amason:gandalf, svargas:phest1945). Passwords are stored in plaintext with no hashing, and are exposed in source code.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password
CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

extra.message: The response header X-XSS-Protection is explicitly set to 0 on every response, disabling the browser's built-in XSS filter. This makes XSS attacks easier to exploit in browsers that support this header.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: The Content-Type header's charset parameter is controlled by user input via params.get("charset", "utf8"). An attacker can inject arbitrary values into the Content-Type header by supplying a crafted ?charset= parameter value. If the value contains newlines, this constitutes HTTP header injection (response splitting). At minimum, forcing an unexpected charset can enable XSS via charset-sniffing attacks.

cwe: CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Response Splitting')

extra.message: In the exception handler at lines 71-73, the full traceback (traceback.format_exc()) is returned as the HTTP response body with a 500 status code. This leaks internal file paths, module names, code fragments, and other debugging information to the client, helping attackers understand the server's internals.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
